column1: # BENCH: Cohere 100K, dim=768, GT=brute-force, seed=42, nq=100, warmup=10
# Index: NSW m_max=32, ef_c=200, cosine, entry_set=64
# Score distribution: min=4.96 p25=11.09 p50=13.83 mean=14.50 p75=18.51 max=28.24
# 1. The <12 bucket from v0.1 was hiding two very different populations:
#    - >=8 (24q): recall=0.929 at ef=200 — moderately hard, NOT helped by ef>200
#    - <8  (11q): recall=0.942 at ef=200 — actually BETTER than >=8 despite lower score!
#      With Ada-ef floor=250: recall=0.954, avg_ef=250, blk=252, p99=15.5ms (warm)
# 2. p99 TAIL is clearly from <8 bucket:
#    warm-ada overall p99=14.0ms, <8 bucket p99=15.5ms (vs baseline <8: 11.1ms)
#    The 11 hardest queries at ef=250 add ~4ms tail — this IS the price of extra budget
# 3. Surprise: >=8 bucket is the ACTUAL recall bottleneck (0.929), not <8 (0.942)
#    These 24 queries drag overall recall down and DON'T benefit from ef>200
#    → Graph quality / entry point problem, not budget problem
# 4. Ada-ef <8 GAINS are real: 0.942 → 0.954 (+0.012) with 50 extra blocks
#    Worth it for recall, but must accept p99 increase
#   bucket     n  recall  blk/q avg_ef  p99ms  maxms
#   bucket     n  recall  blk/q avg_ef  p99ms  maxms
